title: Fit Check Call
meeting_title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Leonid Shvorob and Mike Grass

transcript.text: Every night, you looks like having a busy day as well.
Yes, very busy day.
So I think you sent the invite for 40 minutes.
I hope it's not going to take that long.
Yeah.
If it's a feat, it maybe takes 30 minutes.
It's not a feat just.
Okay.
Yeah.
Take a look.
Okay.
Yeah.
So, yeah, tell me, please, like, why you decide jumping on this call?
Like, what's changing in your processes that you're needing new plans in this quarter?
Yeah, we're looking to grow and get more business and stuff like that, of course.
We, okay, that's fine.
We started out in 2020, January of 2020, right before the pandemic kind of hit the U.S.
and everything.
So that was fun.
Ended up closing our offices and working remotely from home for a couple of years.
And we've got new offices we've been in for about a year and a half and looking to grow our team.
But you've got to have the work to start.
Support that growth.
And so definitely looking for more business and more opportunities to do what we do.
And if you're not familiar with our company, what we do is mostly direct placement for management level professionals in construction and engineering, some architectural stuff.
But, you know, we find candidates that our clients can consider for hire.
And if they make a successful hire, they pay us a fee.
So we incur a lot of the upfront costs of, you know, recruiting and finding the people and that kind of stuff.
But less headaches as far as, you know, once they hire those people, they're off and working for them directly and we just send them a bill.
yeah, looking for more, you know, people, companies that are looking to hire the type of roles that we recruit for and, you know.
Yeah, gotcha.
So, yeah, you mentioned on website, LinkedIn, that you're more like a staffing or you're not focused on this anymore.
Um, not like...
Temp Staffing, mean, we have offered Temp or Temp to Perm, but we typically sell away from it, especially in a market where it's hard to find candidates.

If you're pitching somebody on, you know, go work Temp to Perm for a year or nine months or three months or whatever the case might be, you know, a lot of people already have other companies interested in hiring them directly, you know, so it's hard to be competitive to hire somebody.
And people will typically keep their resume out there.
If they're hired on a Temp to Perm, there's no commitment that it's going to work out, so they'll keep their resume out there, keep their options open and that kind of thing.
So we can do that, but it's not our preference and we kind of try to sell away from it at this time, you know, so sometimes it makes sense.

So sometimes people are looking for think it's like more operational, like headache and so, so what's like exact roles give you now like most profit, more profit and like more easy to feel consistently?
Like you say, it's like management in like construction firms or what kind of thing?
So like.

On-site management, like superintendents, foremen, project engineers, the office staff is project managers, senior project managers, project executives, VPs of operation, estimators, you know, those kind of roles.
We've recruited for, like, front desk associates and, you know, just administrative people and stuff like that, too.
So, we can recruit for anything.
can recruit for IT professionals, but, you know, we try to have a kind of a niche in our market.
Yeah, yeah.
You know, usually I'm not working with clients who are just doing everything for everyone.
It's more difficult to fulfill, you know?
Yes, yes.
So, yeah, makes sense.
So, like, what kind of, like, ideal companies?
It's, like, small businesses or, like, SMBs or what size of, like, headcounts?
Good question.
So, typically, like, midsize, and I guess you could define that a few ways, but what we found is that...

The smaller kind of mom-and-pop shops or the startups, the, you know, companies that aren't well-established, maybe 20 employees or less, they don't, you know, they might need to hire, but they don't have the budget to pay the kind of fees we charge to hire people.
And so even when we work with them, it's often, here's a great candidate, you should hire them.
Like, there's hesitancy because if they make a hire, then they're going to have to pay the fee and stuff like that.
So it's kind of like really frustrating trying to, or they're very picky because it's such a big deal to pay the fee, you know, for placement.

The biggest, the big, companies out there, we work with some really big companies that I'm proud to say we've signed up, but, you know, they often have, you know, a team of recruiters, 10, 15, 20 recruiters that work for their company already.

So how much can me and my small team help them, you know, like it's, and sometimes it's a lot of who to deal with and kind of, you know, a lot of systems that are, you know, less than optimum for them to make a decision because you're dealing with, thing thing can do How So how
who's maybe has to then get something approved and then, you know, it's a whole.
Yeah, it's like bureaucracy.
So as I say, like from 50 to 100 is like sweet spot for you.
Yeah, I think so.
Companies that maybe don't have more than one person or any people that are directly, you know, in charge of recruiting, don't have their system set up for full-time recruiting.
Some companies that they don't, they're not constantly hiring, you know, some companies are so big that they're always hiring for some sort of role.
Some companies are just, you know, once in a while they lose somebody or they need to hire for a role and that kind of thing.
And they don't have, you know, their own system set up and their own full-time recruiting.
And so they, you have, you know, the HR lady or the owner of the company, you know, kind of recruit and place ads and stuff.
Just let us do that.
We can specialize in this.

Yeah, so you mentioned, yeah, so like small businesses, they don't have so much money to fees, so it's like you work in more retained or contingency as well?

Um, we're, it's all contingent on them making a hire, so yeah, we don't charge them anything until they make a hire, so we do incur the costs of, you know, various job platforms that we advertise on, recruiting that we, you know, source resumes from, that kind of stuff, paying recruiters to, to find candidates, invent them out, and stuff like that, so, you know, like, yeah, like, Okay, new client, and they have, you know, five different jobs they're looking to fill, that's great, but I start incurring a lot of costs, and it could be months before I get any money out of.

So yeah, so like, if it's like contingency, is it like typical that you competing with other recruiters and sometimes we'll negotiate that I had a prospective client reach out and I know she's, she owns a business in Richmond, Virginia, and she's been using other recruiters for months now and not finding the right person.
And so she asked me to, you know, work with her and said, Okay, here's my fees.

And I want this and I want want that.

And what do you want me to do?
So they think it makes my job harder, not easier.
So why am I giving you, you know, lower rates, you know, so, but you know, that's okay.
So we turn them down.
Okay, got you.

So you have

Right, okay, so as I mentioned, know, we started out in kind of 2020, but we had the pandemic and stuff, but we, by we, me, and my first hire, which is my head of business development, that he and I, he worked for me in the past at a previous company and stuff, and both of us have a lot of connections in construction and engineering, and especially in the Washington, D.C.
metro area.

and you went to other, like, states, so it's, like, different markets and more...

client, you know, but new job opportunities, new orders or recs, as we call it, you know, we could certainly do 10 to 20 new recs, whether that's with one big client or with 20 different clients, it wouldn't matter to me.

If you want, I can send you a proposal, I think tomorrow.
Okay.
So, because now it's a bit late.
Yeah, yeah, get some sleep.
Do you have like WhatsApp number if you don't mind?
So it's, I think, more easy to communicate.
What's up?
Oh, yeah.
My phone number, if you have it, it's, I think it's on my email, if we've emailed before.
And that's also my number on WhatsApp.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Mike, so I will send you a proposal, I think, tomorrow, the after tomorrow.
So ideally, where do you want to get things going?
Probably the next 30 days, 30, 45 days, something like that.
You know, probably get through January, mid-February, something like that to get rolling.
Yeah, that's good.
So usually, for clients, we start working.
We already have all infrastructure setting up, so it doesn't need to wait in, like, a lot of time.
So usually, it's, like, two or three weeks to get in first intros, and then it's just usually getting higher.
Okay.
All right.
And would you ever consider doing like a fee for lead as far as not have like a monthly, you know, just kind of like a performance-based fee per cost, you know, per lead, that kind of thing?
Because I've been offered that too.
And I don't know if that's better because it's hard to budget that, you know?
Yeah, so...
All these leads, I don't need that many.
Like, no, you have to pay us for them, you know?
Yeah.
Yeah, we start from like 90 days pilot and after if need some restructuring, so we can discuss it.
Okay, all right.
Sounds fair.
All right.
Thanks, Leonid.
Appreciate it.
Get some sleep.
Yeah, no, I have a lot of stuff going on right now, so it's like three hours more.
Right, right.
But yeah, thank you.
Have a great rest of your day, Mike.
You too.
Thanks.
Bye-bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate a lead-gen partnership to scale a recruiting business.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=724.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Goal:** Scale by adding 10–20 new job orders ("recs") per quarter, as the existing network of contacts is exhausted.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=587.0)
  - [**Target:** Contingent direct placement for management roles in mid-size (50–100 employee) construction/engineering firms.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0)
  - [**Solution:** A 90-day pilot for $9k ($3k/mo) guarantees 10–15 pre-qualified introductions to decision-makers.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1459.0)
  - [**Value Prop:** SystemHustle uses market signals to identify hiring urgency, securing introductions to roles *before* they go public, which avoids competition and fee negotiation.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=845.0)

## Topics

### Problem: Scaling Beyond the Network

  - [**Business Model:** Contingent direct placement for management roles in construction, engineering, and architecture.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=66.0)
  - [**Target Client:** Mid-size firms (50–100 employees) that lack an in-house recruiting team but have a budget for fees.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0)
      - [**Avoids:** Small startups (budget constraints) and large enterprises (internal recruiters, bureaucracy).](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0)
  - [**Growth Constraint:** The initial network of contacts in the D.C. metro area is exhausted, requiring a new, predictable client acquisition system.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=474.0)
  - [**Financial Pressure:** The contingent model creates a cash-flow lag, as costs are incurred for months before a placement fee is earned.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=386.0)

### Solution: SystemHustle's Targeted Outreach

  - [**Core Strategy:** Use market signals (e.g., project awards, headcount growth) to identify firms with immediate hiring needs.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=845.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=845.0)
    1.  [**Identify Urgency:** Pinpoint firms needing to hire *now*.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=845.0)
    2.  [**Secure Introduction:** Contact decision-makers and frame Blueprints as a trusted partner.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1123.0)
    3.  [**Deliver Qualified Intro:** Provide a warm introduction to a pre-vetted prospect ready to discuss hiring.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1047.0)
  - [**Benefit:** Access to roles before they are posted publicly, avoiding competition and fee negotiation.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=912.0)

### Proposal: 90-Day Pilot

  - [**Terms:** $3,000/month for a 90-day pilot ($9,000 total).](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1459.0)
  - [**Guarantee:** 10–15 pre-qualified introductions over the 90-day period.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1459.0)
  - [**Rationale:** The 90-day term allows time for feedback and system optimization, as monthly volume can fluctuate.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1685.0)
  - [**Post-Pilot:** The engagement can continue at $3,000/month or be restructured (e.g., fee-share) to target higher-ROI clients.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1553.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Leonid:** Send a formal proposal to Michael via WhatsApp.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1901.0)
  - [**Michael:** Review the proposal and decide on a start date within the next 30–45 days.](https://fathom.video/share/-BAATx7si6F6ojR_9QywW2jvrhNGArVq?tab=summary&timestamp=1947.0)

action_items.description: Send proposal to Michael re: 90-day pilot ($3k/mo, 10–15 intros); request review/decision
